info.title: STEAMI API
info.description:
STEAMI Backend — articles, insights, chat, feed, explainers, research, blog, diary, dashboard, newsletter, Google auth, CMS.

**Test Accounts (POST /api/auth/login):**
- Admin: `admin@steami.dev` / `Admin@steami123`
- Mod:   `mod@steami.dev`   / `Mod@steami123`
- User:  `user@steami.dev`  / `User@steami123`

Paste the `token` value into **Authorize -> Bearer <token>** above.

**Google Auth:** POST /api/auth/google with `{"id_token": "<google-id-token>"}`

**Newsletter:** POST /api/newsletter/send-daily to send the daily digest.

**Article Fetch:** Mod/admin only. After fetch, AI insights are generated automatically in the background (2-3 min gap between each).

**Feed:** Text-selection feed is public. Feed insights auto-generate in background.

**Blog:** Full CRUD at `/api/blog` — seed via POST /api/blog/seed (admin).

**CMS:** GET /api/cms/explainers|research|blog for edit-form-ready docs (mod/admin).

**AI Agents:** GET /api/public/ai-context for full context, POST /api/newsletter/ai-subscribe to subscribe users.

**Security:** DDoS protection active. Admin can manage bans at `GET /api/security/stats`.

get.description:
GET /api/security/stats
Returns real-time DDoS protection statistics.
Shows request counts, blocks, rate-limit hits, active bans.
ADMIN ONLY.

post.summary: Permanently ban an IP — admin only
post.description:
POST /api/security/ban/{ip}
Add an IP to the runtime permanent ban list immediately.
This ban persists until the server restarts (add to BANNED_IPS in .env for permanent).
ADMIN ONLY.

delete.description:
DELETE /api/security/ban/{ip}
Remove an IP from both the runtime ban list and temp ban list.
Cannot unban IPs in the BANNED_IPS .env variable (restart required for that).
ADMIN ONLY.

delete.summary: Clear all temporary bans — admin only
delete.description:
DELETE /api/security/temp-bans
Remove all current temporary bans. Useful after a false-positive flood.
ADMIN ONLY.

post.description:
POST /api/auth/seed
Seed the three dummy accounts if they don't exist yet. Idempotent.
Each account is auto-subscribed to the newsletter on first creation.

POST /api/auth/signup
Register a new user. New users always start with role = "user".

Every new user is automatically subscribed to the newsletter via
POST /api/newsletter/subscribe. The `subscribe_email` field can be
set to false to skip the subscription (defaults to true).

Body: { full_name, email, password, profession, subscribe_email }

Response: { token, user, role }

After signup, call POST /api/auth/interests to choose STEM topics.

post.summary: Login — public, returns token + user + role
post.description:
POST /api/auth/login
Authenticate and receive a JWT token.

Test accounts:
  admin@steami.dev / Admin@steami123  → admin
  mod@steami.dev   / Mod@steami123    → mod
  user@steami.dev  / User@steami123   → user

POST /api/auth/interests
Save the STEM topics this user wants to follow.

get.summary: Get single user by ID — admin only

post.summary: Update email digest subscription — requires auth

get.summary: Get own full profile — requires auth
get.description:
**Requires login.** Get your own full profile.

Returns all fields including profession, bio, interests, newsletter status.

patch.summary: Update profile fields (profession, bio, interests, etc.) — requires auth
patch.description:
**Requires login.** Update profile fields that Google doesn't provide.

All fields are optional — only send the ones you want to change.

Typically called right after Google sign-in for new users (onboarding step).

Body (all optional):
```json
{
  "display_name":          "Jane Doe",
  "profession":            "student",
  "bio":                   "I love STEM!",
  "avatar_url":            "https://...",
  "interests":             ["AI + ROBOTICS", "PHYSICS"],
  "subscribed_newsletter": true
}
```

Profession values: `student` | `researcher` | `educator` | `professional` | `other`

patch.summary: Toggle email subscription on/off — requires auth
Admin toggle subscribe for any user

post.summary: Sign in or sign up with Google ID token — PUBLIC
post.description:
**Public endpoint.** Sign in or create an account using a Google ID token.

The frontend must obtain a Google ID token via the Google Sign-In SDK
(e.g., `google.accounts.id.initialize` / `googleUser.credential`).

Flow:
1. Token is verified with Google's tokeninfo endpoint.
2. If the email already exists in STEAMI → returns existing user + JWT.
3. If the email is new → creates account (role=user) + returns JWT.

Body:
```json
{ "id_token": "<google-id-token>" }
```

Response (same shape as POST /api/auth/login):
```json
{
  "token":        "<steami-jwt>",
  "uid":          "uuid",
  "email":        "user@gmail.com",
  "display_name": "Jane Doe",
  "role":         "user",
  "avatar_url":   "https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/...",
  "is_new_user":  true
}
```

After receiving the token, check `is_new_user`:
- If `true` → redirect to onboarding (PATCH /api/auth/profile to set profession/bio).
- If `false` → redirect to main app.

get.summary: List all newsletter subscribers — ADMIN ONLY

post.summary: Subscribe an email to the newsletter — PUBLIC
Send daily digest to all subscribers via Brevo — ADMIN ONLY

get.summary: Preview today's digest HTML — ADMIN ONLY

post.summary: Send a test digest email to one address — ADMIN ONLY
post.description: Body: { "to_email": "admin@example.com" }

post.summary: AI agent endpoint — subscribe a user — PUBLIC

get.summary: AI agent context — system prompt and API info for AI assistants — PUBLIC
get.description:
**Public.** Returns a structured JSON payload designed to be consumed by
AI assistants visiting {SITE_NAME}. Contains:

- `system_prompt`: ready-to-use system prompt for the AI
- `newsletter_subscribe_endpoint`: how to subscribe users
- `public_api_endpoints`: what the AI can call without auth
- `topics_covered`: STEM domains available on the platform

**Public.** Basic metadata about {SITE_NAME}: name, URL, tagline, topics.
Useful for AI agents that want a quick summary before loading the full context.

post.description: Create or update a user profile. Call on login/register.

get.description:
Get all users except the current user. Optional ?q= username search.
Reads directly from MongoDB `users` collection so every registered user
is always visible — no Firestore chat_users sync required.

Returns ALL users (except the current user) enriched with:
  - unread_count  : how many unread messages they sent to you
  - last_message  : their most recent message exchanged with you (or null)
  - has_chatted   : True if you've exchanged at least one message

Sorted: users you've chatted with come first (by last message timestamp),
then the rest alphabetically — ready to render a full People/Discover list.

Get a single user profile. Checks chat_users for online status,
falls back to MongoDB `users` as the authoritative source.

post.description: Send a message from one user to another.

get.description:
Poll messages between two users.
Pass after=<timestamp_ms> to get only new messages since last poll.
Auto-marks received messages as seen.

patch.description: Mark all messages from senderId → receiverId as seen.

get.description:
Get all conversations for the sidebar.
Returns each chat partner with last message and unread count.
Total unread count + per-sender breakdown. Use for notification badge.

post.description:
POST /api/feed/from-selection  — **PUBLIC**

Called when a user selects text and clicks "Feed".

Returns up to 4 articles. AI insights are generated in the background
(2–3 minute gap between each). The response includes any insights that
are already cached; new ones appear as they are generated.

The frontend should show: *"AI insights are generating — check back in a few minutes."*

Body:
```json
{
  "selected_text":     "Quantum computing uses superposition...",
  "uid":               "user-uuid",
  "source_article_id": "article-uuid"
}
```

Response:
```json
{
  "selected_text":    "...",
  "paragraphs":       [...],
  "keywords":         [...],
  "matched_domains":  [...],
  "source":           "database",
  "total":            3,
  "insights_ready":   1,
  "insights_pending": 2,
  "articles": [ { ..., "has_insight": true/false, "ai_insight": {...} or null } ]
}
```

delete.summary: Delete feed articles and their insights older than N days — MOD/ADMIN ONLY
delete.description:
DELETE /api/feed/cleanup  — **MOD/ADMIN ONLY**

Removes stale feed articles and their corresponding AI insights.

- `days`    — age threshold in days (default 15, min 1, max 365).
- `dry_run` — when `true`, returns the count of articles that *would* be
              deleted without touching the database.

Both `feed_articles` and `ai_insights` documents are removed for each
matched article so no orphaned insights are left behind.

parameters.name: days

schema.description: Delete articles older than this many days
schema.title: Days

parameters.description: Delete articles older than this many days

post.summary: Manually generate insight for a feed article — MOD/ADMIN ONLY
post.description:
POST /api/feed/items/{item_id}/insight  — **MOD/ADMIN ONLY**

Manually generate (or re-generate) an AI insight for a feed article.
Use this only to fix articles whose automatic insight generation failed.

Normally, insights are generated automatically after feed/from-selection.

Pass ?force=true to bypass the cache and regenerate.

post.summary: Save a diary entry — requires auth
post.description:
POST /api/diary
Save selected content from any popup to the personal diary.
The source document is fetched from MongoDB at save time and embedded
as `source_doc` so the diary entry is self-contained even if the
original is later deleted.

Body:
{
  "popup_type":    "ai_insight",
  "popup_id":      "article-uuid-here",
  "title":         "AI Health Tools Article",
  "selected_text": "AI health chatbots are rapidly gaining popularity...",
  "note":          "Interesting point about lack of clinical testing"
}

popup_type options:
  research_article | ai_insight | explainer | simulation

Response:
{
  "id":            "diary-entry-uuid",
  "uid":           "user-uuid",
  "popup_type":    "ai_insight",
  "popup_id":      "article-uuid",
  "title":         "AI Health Tools Article",
  "selected_text": "...",
  "note":          "Interesting point...",
  "source_doc":    { ...snapshot of the source document... },
  "created_at":    "2026-04-09T...",
  "updated_at":    "2026-04-09T..."
}

curl -X POST http://127.0.0.1:5000/api/diary \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer <token>" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"popup_type":"explainer","popup_id":"quantum-dog","title":"Quantum Dog","selected_text":"Superposition allows..."}'

get.summary: List own diary entries — requires auth
get.description:
GET /api/diary?limit=50&popup_type=ai_insight
List all diary entries for the current user, newest first.
Optional filter by popup_type.

Response:
{
  "entries": [ { ...diary entry... }, ... ],
  "total":   12
}

curl -H "Authorization: Bearer <token>" http://127.0.0.1:5000/api/diary
curl -H "Authorization: Bearer <token>" "http://127.0.0.1:5000/api/diary?popup_type=explainer"

get.summary: Get single diary entry — requires auth
get.description:
GET /api/diary/{entry_id}
Get a specific diary entry. Only the owner (or admin) can access it.

If the entry's source_doc snapshot is stale or missing, a fresh copy
is fetched from MongoDB and returned alongside the entry under
`live_source_doc` (the stored entry is not mutated).

curl -H "Authorization: Bearer <token>" http://127.0.0.1:5000/api/diary/ENTRY_ID

put.summary: Update diary entry note/title — requires auth
put.description:
PUT /api/diary/{entry_id}
Update the personal note or title on a diary entry.
Only the owner can update their own entries.

Body: { "note": "Updated annotation", "title": "New title" }

curl -X PUT http://127.0.0.1:5000/api/diary/ENTRY_ID \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer <token>" \
  -d '{"note":"Updated thought"}'

delete.description:
DELETE /api/diary/{entry_id}
Delete a diary entry. Only the owner (or admin) can delete it.

curl -X DELETE http://127.0.0.1:5000/api/diary/ENTRY_ID \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer <token>"

post.summary: Log popup open event — requires auth
post.description:
POST /api/dashboard/event
Called by the frontend every time a user opens any popup.

Body:
{
  "popup_type":  "explainer",
  "popup_id":    "quantum-dog",
  "popup_title": "The Quantum Dog: Schrödinger's Pet Paradox"
}

get.description:
GET /api/dashboard/me
Returns the current user's full dashboard data:
- recent events list (with date and time)
- counts per popup type
- counts per day (for a simple calendar/streak view)
- most opened items
- user interests (from profile)
- insight stats (total insights available, with_insight count)

Response:
{
  "total_events": 42,
  "by_type": { "explainer": 15, "ai_insight": 20, ... },
  "by_date": { "2026-04-09": 8, ... },
  "most_opened": [ { "popup_id": "...", "popup_title": "...", "popup_type": "...", "count": 5 } ],
  "recent": [ { ...event... }, ... ],
  "interests": ["PHYSICS", "AI + ROBOTICS"],
  "insight_stats": {
    "total_insights": 32,
    "articles_with_insight": 28,
    "articles_total": 40,
    "generating": false
  },
  "diary_total": 7
}

get.summary: Per-subject engagement scores for the SubjectRadarChart — requires auth
get.description:
GET /api/dashboard/subject-intelligence

Powers the Subject Intelligence radar chart on the dashboard.

For every popup event the user has opened, this endpoint resolves the
STEAMI subject/field of that content item (by looking it up in the
`explainers`, `research_articles`, or `ai_insights` Firestore collections)
and tallies how many times the user engaged with each subject.

Scores are normalised to 0–100.  A user's saved interests receive a small
base bonus (5 pts) so they show up even before any content has been opened.

All 12 STEAMI subjects are always returned so the radar chart always has
a full set of axes.

Response:
{
  "subjects": [
    {
      "subject":     "PHYSICS",
      "opens":       14,
      "score":       95,
      "is_interest": true
    },
    ...                          // all 12 subjects, sorted by score desc
  ],
  "total_events_analysed": 87,
  "top_subject": "PHYSICS",
  "user_interests": ["PHYSICS", "AI + ROBOTICS"]
}

GET /api/dashboard/admin
Platform-wide activity statistics. ADMIN ONLY.

GET /api/dashboard/admin/events
Raw paginated event log. ADMIN ONLY.

post.summary: Generate AI insight for an article — MOD / ADMIN only
post.description:
**POST /api/articles/{article_id}/generate-insight**

Triggers on-demand AI insight generation for the specified article.

- Requires **mod** or **admin** role (HTTP 403 for plain users).
- Returns the **cached insight** when `has_insight` is already `true`,
  unless `?force=true` is supplied to force a re-generation.
- Writes the result back to both `articles` and `ai_insights` collections,
  mirroring the behaviour of the background insight queue in `main.py`.

**Example request:**
```
POST /api/articles/abc123/generate-insight
Authorization: Bearer <mod_or_admin_token>
```

**Force re-generation:**
```
POST /api/articles/abc123/generate-insight?force=true
Authorization: Bearer <admin_token>
```

parameters.name: force

schema.description: Set to true to regenerate the insight even if one already exists. Defaults to false — returns cached insight when available.
schema.title: Force

parameters.description: Set to true to regenerate the insight even if one already exists. Defaults to false — returns cached insight when available.

200.description: The generated (or cached) AI insight together with article metadata.

get.description:
Returns the authenticated user's full profile (password hash excluded).

Frontend usage:
  GET /api/profile/me
  Authorization: Bearer <token>

patch.summary: Update basic profile info — requires auth
patch.description:
Update one or more profile fields in a single request.
Only send the fields you want to change — all others are left untouched.

Fields you can update here:
  full_name, username, bio, location, website, profession,
  interests, subscribe_email

Frontend usage:
  PATCH /api/profile/me
  Authorization: Bearer <token>
  Content-Type: application/json
  { "full_name": "Sahil Sharma", "bio": "AI enthusiast 🚀" }

delete.summary: Delete own account — requires auth + password

patch.summary: Change email address — requires auth + password
patch.description:
Change the user's email address.
Requires the current password to confirm identity.
The new email must not already be in use by another account.

Frontend usage:
  PATCH /api/profile/me/email
  Authorization: Bearer <token>
  { "new_email": "newemail@example.com", "current_password": "MyPass123" }

patch.summary: Change password — requires auth + current password
patch.description:
Change the user's password.
Requires the current password.
New password must be ≥8 chars with uppercase, lowercase, and a digit.

Frontend usage:
  PATCH /api/profile/me/password
  Authorization: Bearer <token>
  { "current_password": "OldPass1", "new_password": "NewPass2025!" }

delete.description:
Remove the user's avatar (sets avatar_url to null).

Frontend usage:
  DELETE /api/profile/me/avatar
  Authorization: Bearer <token>

patch.summary: Set or update avatar via image URL — requires auth
patch.description:
Set or replace the user's avatar using an image URL.

Accepted URLs:
  • Direct image links ending in .jpg .jpeg .png .gif .webp .avif .svg
  • Links from trusted hosts: Imgur, Cloudinary, Gravatar, GitHub, Google, etc.

Example accepted URLs:
  https://i.imgur.com/abc123.jpg
  https://res.cloudinary.com/demo/image/upload/sample.jpg
  https://www.gravatar.com/avatar/abc123?s=200
  https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/12345678
  https://example.com/photos/myface.png

Frontend usage:
  PATCH /api/profile/me/avatar
  Authorization: Bearer <token>
  { "avatar_url": "https://i.imgur.com/abc123.jpg" }

post.summary: Manually trigger article/feed cleanup — ADMIN ONLY
post.description:
ADMIN ONLY — immediately run one cleanup pass without waiting for
the next scheduled 24-hour window.

Deletes articles and their AI insights older than 15 days, and
feed items and their AI insights older than 15 days.

get.summary: Check cleanup scheduler status — ADMIN ONLY
get.description: ADMIN ONLY — check whether the cleanup scheduler is running.

post.description: POST /api/images/upload?folder=explainers Upload any image to the server's images// directory. The returned `url` value is what you store in the MongoDB `image` field and use as the `` value in the frontend. Allowed folders: research | explainers | blog Deduplication: if the uploaded bytes match an existing file anywhere on disk, the existing URL is returned without creating a new file. Allowed types: JPEG, PNG, WebP, GIF Response: { "url": "/images/explainers/my-photo.jpg", "filename": "my-photo.jpg", "folder": "explainers" } curl -X POST "http://127.0.0.1:5000/api/images/upload?folder=explainers" \ -H "Authorization: Bearer " \ -F "file=@/path/to/image.jpg"

POST /api/explainers/seed
Bulk-seed all 9 explainers from content_data.py.
Safe to re-run (uses MongoDB upsert — never duplicates).
Image URLs in seed data point to files that must exist in images/explainers/.

curl -X POST http://127.0.0.1:5000/api/explainers/seed \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer <admin_token>"

POST /api/explainers/create-with-image  (multipart/form-data)
Create a new explainer AND upload its image in a single request.

The image is saved as  images/explainers/<id>.<ext>
and the URL is stored automatically in the `image` field of the new MongoDB doc.

Form fields:
  image        — file upload (required)
  id           — unique string ID, e.g. "quantum-dog"  (required)
  title        — explainer title  (required)
  subtitle     — short subtitle
  field        — STEM field, e.g. "QUANTUM PHYSICS"
  badgeColor   — e.g. "cyan", "green", "violet"
  readTime     — e.g. "8 MIN READ"
  content      — JSON string: '["Para 1...", "Para 2..."]'
  keyInsights  — JSON string: '["Insight 1...", "Insight 2..."]'

Example curl:
  curl -X POST http://127.0.0.1:5000/api/explainers/create-with-image \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer <mod_token>" \
    -F "image=@/path/to/quantum-dog.jpg" \
    -F "id=quantum-dog" \
    -F "title=The Quantum Dog" \
    -F 'content=["Paragraph one...", "Paragraph two..."]' \
    -F 'keyInsights=["Insight one", "Insight two"]'

POST /api/explainers  (JSON body)
Create a new explainer.

Pass `image` as the URL returned by POST /api/images/upload,
or leave it empty and call POST /api/explainers/{id}/image afterwards.

curl -X POST http://127.0.0.1:5000/api/explainers \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer <mod_token>" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"id":"my-explainer","title":"My Title","field":"AI","image":"/images/explainers/my.jpg"}'

get.description:
GET /api/explainers?field=BIOLOGY
List all explainers. Optional field filter. Each item includes the image URL.
No token required.

curl http://127.0.0.1:5000/api/explainers

put.description:
PUT /api/explainers/{id}
Update explainer fields. Pass new image URL to change the image.
All fields are optional — only provided fields are updated.

curl -X PUT http://127.0.0.1:5000/api/explainers/quantum-dog \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer <mod_token>" \
  -d '{"readTime":"10 MIN READ","image":"/images/explainers/new-quantum.jpg"}'

delete.description:
DELETE /api/explainers/{id}
Permanently delete an explainer. Admin only.

curl -X DELETE http://127.0.0.1:5000/api/explainers/quantum-dog \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer <admin_token>"

post.description:
POST /api/explainers/{id}/image  (multipart/form-data)
Upload or replace the image for an existing explainer.

The file is saved as  images/explainers/<explainer_id>.<ext>
and the `image` field in MongoDB is updated automatically.

Response:
{
  "updated":   true,
  "id":        "quantum-dog",
  "image":     "/images/explainers/quantum-dog.jpg"
}

curl -X POST http://127.0.0.1:5000/api/explainers/quantum-dog/image \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer <mod_token>" \
  -F "image=@/path/to/quantum-dog.jpg"

get.description:
GET /api/research/fields
Returns fields list, icons, badge colors, and field image URLs.
Frontend can use field_images to replace hardcoded research-images.ts.

curl http://127.0.0.1:5000/api/research/fields

GET /api/research/images
Returns the complete field → image URL mapping.
Use this to replace the hardcoded research-images.ts import.

curl http://127.0.0.1:5000/api/research/images

post.description:
POST /api/research/seed
Bulk-seed all 11 research articles from content_data.py.
Safe to re-run.

curl -X POST http://127.0.0.1:5000/api/research/seed \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer <admin_token>"

POST /api/research/articles/create-with-image  (multipart/form-data)
Create a new research article AND upload its image in one request.

Each research article gets its own image file.
The image is saved as  images/research/<id>.<ext>
Deduplication is applied — identical bytes reuse the existing file.

Form fields:
  image          — file upload (required)
  id             — unique string ID  (required)
  title          — article title  (required)
  field          — research field e.g. "PHYSICS"  (required)
  abstract       — short abstract
  author         — author name
  date           — YYYY-MM-DD
  readTime       — e.g. "10 min"
  content        — JSON string: '["Para 1...", "Para 2..."]'
  quotes         — JSON string: '["Quote 1...", "Quote 2..."]'
  keyFindings    — JSON string: '["Finding 1...", "Finding 2..."]'
  relatedTopics  — JSON string: '["Topic 1...", "Topic 2..."]'

Example curl:
  curl -X POST http://127.0.0.1:5000/api/research/articles/create-with-image \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer <mod_token>" \
    -F "image=@/path/to/physics.jpg" \
    -F "id=a99" \
    -F "title=New Physics Discovery" \
    -F "field=PHYSICS" \
    -F 'content=["Para 1...", "Para 2..."]'

POST /api/research/articles  (JSON body)
Create a research article.

curl -X POST http://127.0.0.1:5000/api/research/articles \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer <mod_token>" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"id":"a99","title":"New Discovery","field":"PHYSICS","image":"/images/research/physics.jpg"}'

get.description:
GET /api/research/articles?field=PHYSICS
List all research articles. Each item includes image URL.
Response also includes field_images map.

curl http://127.0.0.1:5000/api/research/articles

GET /api/research/articles/{id}

curl http://127.0.0.1:5000/api/research/articles/a1

put.description:
PUT /api/research/articles/{id}
Update fields. Pass new image URL to change the image.

curl -X PUT http://127.0.0.1:5000/api/research/articles/a1 \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer <mod_token>" \
  -d '{"readTime":"15 min"}'

post.description:
POST /api/research/articles/{id}/image  (multipart/form-data)
Upload or replace the image for a specific research article.

Each research article has its own image (saved as images/research/<id>.<ext>).
Uploading the same image bytes that already exist anywhere on disk will reuse
the existing file — no duplicate is created.

Response:
{
  "updated":     true,
  "id":          "a1",
  "image":       "/images/research/a1.jpg",
  "deleted_old": false
}

curl -X POST http://127.0.0.1:5000/api/research/articles/a1/image \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer <mod_token>" \
  -F "image=@/path/to/a1.jpg"

POST /api/blog/seed
ADMIN ONLY — bulk-seed all blog posts from content_data.BLOG_POSTS_SEED.
Safe to re-run (MongoDB upsert — never duplicates).

curl -X POST http://127.0.0.1:5000/api/blog/seed \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer <admin_token>"

POST /api/blog  (JSON body)
Create a new blog post. Set coverImage to a URL, or leave empty and
upload via POST /api/blog/{id}/cover-image afterwards.

curl -X POST http://127.0.0.1:5000/api/blog \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer <mod_token>" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"id":"my-post","title":"My Post","field":"AI","type":"article"}'

get.description:
GET /api/blog?field=BIOLOGY&type=article&tag=CRISPR
List all blog posts. Supports optional filtering by field, type, and tag.

curl http://127.0.0.1:5000/api/blog
curl "http://127.0.0.1:5000/api/blog?field=AI&type=article"

parameters.name: type

schema.description: Filter by type: article | explainer | simulation
schema.title: Type

parameters.description: Filter by type: article | explainer | simulation

get.description:
GET /api/blog/{id}

curl http://127.0.0.1:5000/api/blog/the-future-of-quantum-computing

put.description:
PUT /api/blog/{id}
Update any fields of a blog post. Only supplied fields are changed.

curl -X PUT http://127.0.0.1:5000/api/blog/the-future-of-quantum-computing \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer <mod_token>" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"title":"Updated Title","badgeColor":"violet"}'

delete.description:
DELETE /api/blog/{id}
ADMIN ONLY.

curl -X DELETE http://127.0.0.1:5000/api/blog/my-post \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer <admin_token>"

post.description:
POST /api/blog/{id}/cover-image  (multipart/form-data)
Upload or replace the cover image for a blog post.
The file is saved as  images/blog/<post_id>.<ext>

curl -X POST http://127.0.0.1:5000/api/blog/my-post/cover-image \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer <mod_token>" \
  -F "image=@/path/to/cover.jpg"

get.description:
GET /api/cms/explainers
MOD/ADMIN — slim list of all explainers for a CMS management table.
Returns id, title, field, badgeColor, readTime, author, image, updated_at.

curl -H "Authorization: Bearer <mod_token>" \
  http://127.0.0.1:5000/api/cms/explainers

GET /api/cms/explainers/{id}
MOD/ADMIN — full explainer document, pre-populated for an edit form.
Includes all fields: content, keyInsights, context, technicalDetail, impact.

curl -H "Authorization: Bearer <mod_token>" \
  http://127.0.0.1:5000/api/cms/explainers/quantum-dog

GET /api/cms/research
MOD/ADMIN — slim list of all research articles for a CMS management table.
Returns id, title, field, author, date, readTime, image, updated_at.

curl -H "Authorization: Bearer <mod_token>" \
  http://127.0.0.1:5000/api/cms/research

GET /api/cms/research/{id}
MOD/ADMIN — full research article document, pre-populated for an edit form.
Includes all fields: abstract, content, quotes, keyFindings, relatedTopics.

curl -H "Authorization: Bearer <mod_token>" \
  http://127.0.0.1:5000/api/cms/research/a1

GET /api/cms/blog
MOD/ADMIN — slim list of all blog posts for a CMS management table.
Returns id, title, field, type, publishDate, readingTime, tags, coverImage, updated_at.

curl -H "Authorization: Bearer <mod_token>" \
  http://127.0.0.1:5000/api/cms/blog

GET /api/cms/blog/{id}
MOD/ADMIN — full blog post document, pre-populated for an edit form.
Includes all fields: content (markdown), author object, keyInsights, tags, etc.

curl -H "Authorization: Bearer <mod_token>" \
  http://127.0.0.1:5000/api/cms/blog/the-future-of-quantum-computing

post.summary: Fetch up to 40 articles by domain — MOD/ADMIN. Auto-generates insights in background.
post.description:
POST /api/articles/refresh  — MOD/ADMIN

Fetches fresh articles from 4 primary RSS sources (MIT Tech Review,
BBC Tech, NYTimes Tech, ScienceDaily). Saves only new articles (skips
duplicates by URL). Then automatically starts generating AI insights
in a background thread with a 2-3 minute pause between each.

Body (all optional):
  { "domains": ["AI + ROBOTICS", "PHYSICS"], "target": 40 }

Response includes insight_thread=true when background generation starts.
Poll GET /api/articles/insights/status to track progress.

get.summary: Articles filtered by your saved interests — requires auth
get.description:
GET /api/articles/for-me?limit=30
Returns articles matching the current user's saved topic interests.
If no interests are saved, returns all recent articles.

post.summary: Fetch articles from RSS by topic — requires mod/admin
post.description: Trigger an RSS fetch by topic/keywords. Requires mod/admin.

post.summary: Fetch from a URL — requires mod/admin
post.description: Fetch articles from a user-supplied URL. Requires mod/admin.

get.description: Public: list all articles, newest first.

post.description: Create an article manually. Requires mod/admin.

get.description: Public: get a single article by ID.

PUBLIC. Returns how many articles have AI insights vs total.
Use this for a real-time progress indicator: "18 of 32 insights ready".
Also shows whether the background generation thread is currently running.

delete.description:
Clear the cached AI insight. Also deletes from ai_insights collection
and removes from insight_queue so it re-queues on next refresh.
Requires mod or admin.

get.description: List all AI insights, newest first. Requires any valid login.
Get a single AI insight by article ID. Requires any valid login.

post.summary: Fetch + generate insights synchronously — requires mod/admin
post.description: Fetch articles and immediately generate AI insights. Requires mod/admin.

post.summary: Manually retry failed/pending insights — MOD/ADMIN ONLY
post.description:
POST /api/articles/insights/process — MOD/ADMIN ONLY

Manually process the next N articles from the insight_queue.
Use this ONLY to fix articles whose automatic background generation failed.

Normally insights are generated automatically after POST /api/articles/refresh.

Body (optional): { "batch_size": 2 }

get.summary: Check insight queue status — ADMIN ONLY
get.description: ADMIN ONLY — check the current state of the insight generation queue.

delete.description: ADMIN ONLY — delete all items from the insight_queue. Use before a fresh refresh.

get.summary: Check for new articles in DB — any auth (read-only)
get.description:
GET /api/articles/refresh/check?since_hours=24
Any authenticated user — check if new articles have been added recently.
Does NOT trigger any RSS fetch.

image.title: Image
image.description: Hero image file for this explainer

image.title: Image
image.description: Hero image for this research article

keyFindings.title: Keyfindings
keyFindings.description: JSON array of key finding strings

image.title: Image
image.description: New image file for this explainer

CreateDiaryBody.title: CreateDiaryBody
CreateDiaryBody.description:
Save a piece of content to the diary.

popup_type    — type of popup where the content was saved from
popup_id      — the ID of the source item (research article ID, insight ID, etc.)
title         — short label for this entry (e.g. article title)
selected_text — the actual text the user highlighted / wants to save
note          — optional personal note the user adds

CreateExplainerBody.title: CreateExplainerBody
CreateExplainerBody.description:
JSON body for POST /api/explainers.
Pass image as URL returned by POST /api/images/upload, or leave empty
and upload later via POST /api/explainers/{id}/image.

PatchProfileBody.title: PatchProfileBody
PatchProfileBody.description: Body for PATCH /api/auth/profile — all fields optional

PopupEventBody.title: PopupEventBody
PopupEventBody.description:
Sent by the frontend every time a popup is opened.
popup_title is optional but helps the dashboard display nicely.

RefreshBody.title: RefreshBody
RefreshBody.description:
Body for POST /api/articles/refresh (MOD/ADMIN).
domains: list of domain names to include (empty = all 10).
target:  how many articles to fetch (max 40, default 40).

SelectionBody.title: SelectionBody
SelectionBody.description: Body sent when user clicks the Feed button after selecting text.

UpdateBlogPostBody.title: UpdateBlogPostBody
UpdateBlogPostBody.description: All fields optional — only provided fields are updated.

UpdateDiaryBody.title: UpdateDiaryBody
UpdateDiaryBody.description: Only the personal note and title can be updated after creation.

UpdateExplainerBody.title: UpdateExplainerBody
UpdateExplainerBody.description: All fields optional — only provided fields are updated.

UpdateProfileBody.title: UpdateProfileBody
UpdateProfileBody.description:
PATCH /api/profile/me
All fields are optional — only the fields you send will be updated.
